commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair is needed; the PR is ready for ordinary maintainer review and CI rather than an automated fix lane.; Cleared: The final diff only changes reload metadata and tests, preserves default-profile restart behavior, and introduces no supply-chain, credential, permission, or new execution surface concern. (sha=71d0d7391b9eaf24fcfcad5240d9fab35e41d154)
commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces the browser plugin’s broad browser restart prefix with a hot browser.profiles prefix and adds planner/plugin metadata tests for profile, default-profile, service-level, and mixed browser config paths.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows browser.profiles paths match the browser plugin’s broad restart prefix, and the config.patch restart decision is derived from `buildGatewayReloadPlan(...).restartGateway`.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair is needed; the PR is ready for ordinary maintainer review and CI rather than an automated fix lane.

target.title: fix(browser): hot-reload browser.profiles and browser.defaultProfile
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem**: The browser plugin registers `restartPrefixes: ["browser"]`, so any `config.patch` (or other write) that changes `browser.profiles.<name>.*` is classified as restart-required by `buildGatewayReloadPlan`. In hot reload mode the gateway then schedules a SIGUSR1 restart even though the runtime already supports hot-reloading profiles via `extensions/browser/src/browser/resolved-config-refresh.ts`.
- **Why it matters**: Skills and tools that rotate sandbox browser profiles via `config.patch` (e.g. setting `browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl` to point at a microVM CDP endpoint) cause an unnecessary full gateway restart, disrupting active agent sessions. Without a supervisor, the gateway also exits foreground. Issue #43803 documents the user-facing impact for the novita-sandbox workflow.
- **What changed**: replaced the broad `restartPrefixes: ["browser"]` with the granular `hotPrefixes: ["browser.profiles"]` in `extensions/browser/plugin-registration.ts`, updated the matching expectation in `extensions/browser/index.test.ts`, and added planner regression tests in `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` (one that locks in the new hot path for `browser.profiles.<name>.*`, one that locks in the preserved restart behavior for `browser.defaultProfile`, and a mixed-paths case that confirms a service path still drives a restart).
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: no changes to the planner itself, the config write flow, the SIGUSR1 scheduling, or the browser runtime. Other `browser.*` paths (`browser.cdpUrl`, `browser.enabled`, `browser.defaultProfile`, ...) keep their existing restart-on-change behavior via the planner's no-rule default branch (`config-reload-plan.ts:365-368`). `browser.defaultProfile` is intentionally NOT hot — `createBrowserRouteContext.forProfile()` in `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.ts` resolves the default-profile name *before* `resolveBrowserProfileWithHotReload()` refreshes resolved config, so a hot reload here would leave the next implicit-profile request routed to the prior default. The planner's prefix-order semantics are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #43803
- Related #5533, #20245 (prior fixes that gated `config.patch` SIGUSR1 by `commands.restart` and skipped restart for no-op reload rules; this PR closes the remaining hot-reloadable-paths gap for the bundled browser plugin's per-profile config).
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `buildGatewayReloadPlan` no longer marks `browser.profiles.<name>.*` as restart-required, so `config.patch` writes that only touch those paths skip `scheduleGatewaySigusr1Restart` in hot reload mode. Service-level browser paths (`browser.cdpUrl`, `browser.enabled`, `browser.defaultProfile`, ...) still trigger a gateway restart through the planner's no-rule default branch.
- **Real environment tested**: local source checkout on Windows 11 (Node 24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0) at the patched HEAD. Ran a Node + tsx script that imports the patched `browserPluginReload` and the real `buildGatewayReloadPlan`, wires the metadata into a fresh `PluginRegistry` via the same path the gateway uses (`setActivePluginRegistry`), and prints the planner output for the paths the linked issue calls out plus regression cases for service-level paths and the intentionally-preserved `browser.defaultProfile` restart.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: from repo root, ran `node --import tsx ./proof.mjs` where `proof.mjs` imports `browserPluginReload`, `buildGatewayReloadPlan`, `setActivePluginRegistry`, `createTestRegistry`, attaches `browserPluginReload` to `registry.reloads`, and prints the planner result (`restartGateway`, `restartReasons`, `hotReasons`) for each of: `browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl`, `browser.profiles.sandbox.userDataDir`, `browser.defaultProfile`, `browser.cdpUrl`, `browser.enabled`, and the mixed set `[browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl, browser.cdpUrl]`.
- **Evidence after fix**: live stdout copied from `node --import tsx ./proof.mjs`; no model invocation involved.
  ```text
  # changedPaths=["browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl"]
  {
    "restartGateway": false,
    "restartReasons": [],
    "hotReasons": [
      "browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl"
    ]
  }

  # changedPaths=["browser.profiles.sandbox.userDataDir"]
  {
    "restartGateway": false,
    "restartReasons": [],
    "hotReasons": [
      "browser.profiles.sandbox.userDataDir"
    ]
  }

  # changedPaths=["browser.defaultProfile"]
  {
    "restartGateway": true,
    "restartReasons": [
      "browser.defaultProfile"
    ],
    "hotReasons": []
  }

  # changedPaths=["browser.cdpUrl"]
  {
    "restartGateway": true,
    "restartReasons": [
      "browser.cdpUrl"
    ],
    "hotReasons": []
  }

  # changedPaths=["browser.enabled"]
  {
    "restartGateway": true,
    "restartReasons": [
      "browser.enabled"
    ],
    "hotReasons": []
  }

  # changedPaths=["browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl","browser.cdpUrl"]
  {
    "restartGateway": true,
    "restartReasons": [
      "browser.cdpUrl"
    ],
    "hotReasons": [
      "browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl"
    ]
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: the bug reproduces only on `main`. With this patch, `browser.profiles.<name>.*` classifies as hot (no `restartGateway`); `browser.cdpUrl`, `browser.enabled`, and `browser.defaultProfile` still classify as restart; a mixed change correctly reports both (hot for the profile path, restart for the service path).
- **What was not tested**: full live gateway with a running novita-sandbox + Chromium that actually issues the failing `config.patch` flow. Not required for the planner-level fix — `shouldScheduleDirectConfigRestart` in `src/gateway/server-methods/config-write-flow.ts:130` derives its restart decision directly from `buildGatewayReloadPlan(...).restartGateway`, and the SIGUSR1 scheduler is gated by that same boolean, so the planner classification is the single decision point this PR needs to flip.
- **Before evidence**: documented in the body of #43803 with redacted gateway logs showing the SIGUSR1 firing 8 s after a `config.patch` of `browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl`.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause**: `extensions/browser/plugin-registration.ts` registered `restartPrefixes: ["browser"]`, which matches every `browser.*` path in `matchRule()` (first-match wins). Per-profile paths inherit that classification even though the browser runtime already hot-reloads them via `resolveBrowserProfileWithHotReload()`.
- **Why this PR does NOT mark `browser.defaultProfile` as hot**: `createBrowserRouteContext.forProfile()` reads `current.resolved.defaultProfile` before invoking `resolveBrowserProfileWithHotReload()`. Marking the default-profile path hot would mean a `config.patch` flipping the default from a host to a sandbox/remote profile could leave the next implicit-profile request routed to the prior default — crossing the operator's intended isolation boundary. Closing that ordering gap belongs in a separate runtime change to `forProfile()`. The planner test for `browser.defaultProfile` carries a comment noting this so a future contributor can remove the restart guard only after fixing the runtime selection order.
- **Missing detection / guardrail**: the existing planner test in `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` only asserted that broad `browser.*` paths trigger a restart, so the lack of a profile-specific hot path was invisible. This PR adds tests that lock in the new classification for `browser.profiles.<name>.*` and explicitly assert that `browser.defaultProfile` still restarts.
- **Contributing context (if known)**: prior PRs already closed two adjacent gaps in this area — `config.patch` no-op reload rules (#13408 → #5533) and `commands.restart` guard for `config.apply`/`config.patch` (#20355 → #20245). The remaining hot-reloadable-paths case for the bundled browser plugin's per-profile config is the third axis those PRs did not address.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file**: `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` (planner classification for `browser.profiles.<name>.*` as hot, `browser.defaultProfile` as still-restart, and the mixed profile + service paths case); `extensions/browser/index.test.ts` (locks the plugin's reload-metadata shape).
- **Scenario the test should lock in**: `browser.profiles.<name>.*` is classified as hot (no `restartGateway`); `browser.defaultProfile` and other `browser.*` paths still trigger a gateway restart; a mixed change containing both still restarts because at least one path requires it.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail**: the planner output is the single decision boundary the config write flow checks before scheduling SIGUSR1. Locking the per-profile hot path, the explicit default-profile restart, and the mixed case is sufficient to prevent regression in either direction.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any)**: the previous test that asserted `browser.enabled` restarts was renamed to "restarts the gateway for service-level browser plugin config changes" so its scope is explicit, and was kept as-is because the new metadata still falls through to a restart for that path.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Skills and tools that change `browser.profiles.<name>.*` via `config.patch` no longer cause a gateway restart in hot reload mode. The browser runtime continues to refresh resolved profiles on demand through `resolved-config-refresh.ts`. Service-level browser config changes (`browser.cdpUrl`, `browser.enabled`, `browser.defaultProfile`, ...) still trigger a restart, as before.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- Browser host/sandbox/remote isolation boundary: unchanged. `browser.defaultProfile` is intentionally NOT hot-reloaded by this PR; see Root Cause for the `forProfile()` ordering rationale.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- OS: Windows 11
- Runtime: Node 24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A (planner-level fix; no model invoked)
- Integration/channel: browser plugin (bundled extension)
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic in-memory `PluginRegistry` populated with `browserPluginReload`; planner exercised with `["browser.profiles.sandbox.cdpUrl"]`, `["browser.defaultProfile"]`, `["browser.cdpUrl"]`, `["browser.enabled"]`, and the mixed pair.

### Steps
1. Apply this patch.
2. From repo root, run a tiny Node + tsx script that imports `browserPluginReload` from `extensions/browser/plugin-registration.ts`, `buildGatewayReloadPlan` from `src/gateway/config-reload-plan.ts`, and wires the metadata into a fresh `PluginRegistry` via `setActivePluginRegistry` + `createTestRegistry`.
3. Call `buildGatewayReloadPlan` with each of the changed-paths cases above.
4. Inspect the returned `restartGateway`, `restartReasons`, and `hotReasons`.

### Expected
- `browser.profiles.<name>.*` → `restartGateway: false`, paths in `hotReasons`.
- `browser.defaultProfile`, `browser.cdpUrl`, `browser.enabled` → `restartGateway: true`, paths in `restartReasons`.
- Mixed `[browser.profiles.<name>.cdpUrl, browser.cdpUrl]` → `restartGateway: true`, both paths classified into their respective buckets.

### Actual
- Matches expected in all six cases (see "Evidence after fix" above).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — the new planner cases in `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` would fail against the old broad `restartPrefixes: ["browser"]` shape; they pass on this patch.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — see Real behavior proof above.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording — N/A (planner-level diff; no UI surface change).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A.

Targeted Vitest runs after the patch (acceptance-criteria files for #43803):

```text
$ pnpm test src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts extensions/browser/index.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/config.shared-auth.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.hot-reload-profiles.test.ts
 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  104 passed (104)
   Duration  ~10s combined
```

Formatting + lint:

```text
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/browser/plugin-registration.ts extensions/browser/index.test.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ node scripts/check-changed.mjs
# exit 0 (no findings)

$ git diff --check
# clean
```

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: the six planner cases listed above, the existing service-level browser case (renamed but otherwise unchanged), and the full `src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts` + `extensions/browser/index.test.ts` files. Also ran the additional acceptance-criteria files (`config.shared-auth.test.ts`, `server-context.hot-reload-profiles.test.ts`) and confirmed they continue to pass against the patched code.
- **Edge cases checked**: a mixed change containing both a profile path and a service path still restarts (driven by the service path). The planner's no-rule fallback (`config-reload-plan.ts:365-368`) preserves the restart for `browser.defaultProfile` and any unknown `browser.*` paths that fall outside the new hot prefix. The plugin's reload-metadata shape test in `index.test.ts` is updated so the new shape is the single source of truth.
- **What I did NOT verify**: full live gateway with novita-sandbox + Chromium and an actual `config.patch` over the gateway RPC; the SIGUSR1-skipping behavior is gated entirely by `buildGatewayReloadPlan(...).restartGateway`, which this PR's tests and proof lock down at the planner boundary.

## Review Conversations
- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes for everything except `browser.profiles.<name>.*`, which is the path #43803 specifically asks to be hot-reloadable. `browser.defaultProfile` and all other `browser.*` paths keep today's restart-on-change behavior.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert: revert the two commits on this branch. The plugin metadata snaps back to `restartPrefixes: ["browser"]`, the new planner tests fail, and `extensions/browser/index.test.ts` expects the old shape again. No data or migration state to roll back.

## Risks and Mitigations

The only behavior change is that `browser.profiles.<name>.*` no longer triggers a gateway restart. The browser runtime already supports refreshing resolved profiles at request time (see `extensions/browser/src/browser/resolved-config-refresh.ts` and the existing `server-context.hot-reload-profiles.test.ts`), so the only operator-visible difference is the absence of the previously-unnecessary restart. Service-level paths and the default-profile alias are unchanged.

## AI-assisted

This PR was prepared with AI assistance. I reviewed the diff, ran the targeted Vitest lanes, oxfmt check, check:changed, and real-behavior proof locally, and confirm the output above is from my machine.
target.author: daoyuanli2816

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
